source.catalog_ref: I-EU7 (I-LA thread collegiate off-road benchmark Brasil)

full_prompt: Design the front lower suspension control arm (A-arm) of a collegiate off-road benchmark Brasil 2026 entry (RATBSB Emenda 7). Vehicle curb mass 275 kg + driver 90 kg. Front weight distribution 42%, so front axle supports 0.42 x 365 = 153 kg, or 77 kg per front wheel. Suspension travel +/- 150 mm. The lower A-arm is welded from AISI 1018 DOM seamless steel tubing, 25.4 mm OD x 1.65 mm wall (secondary structural member per collegiate off-road benchmark T.9.2, given A-arm is secondary). Material: E 200 GPa, yield 370 MPa, ultimate 440 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3. Arm geometry: trapezoidal planform with inner pivot axis length 320 mm, outer ball-joint mount extending 400 mm from inner pivot axis, two tubes forming an A at 30 deg included angle. Load cases (collegiate off-road benchmark structural analysis recommended in RATBSB scrutineering): (LC1) static 1 g on 77 kg corner = 755 N upward at ball-joint; (LC2) bump 3 g: 2266 N vertical at ball-joint; (LC3) cornering 1.5 g lateral + 1 g vertical: 1133 N lateral + 755 N vertical at ball-joint; (LC4) braking 1.2 g longitudinal decel + 1 g vertical: 906 N fore-aft + 755 N vertical at ball-joint (note longitudinal component creates additional couple at inner pivots); (LC5) landing from 1 m drop: 5 g vertical = 3776 N at ball-joint; (LC6) tie-down strap: 500 N static upward on outer ball-joint (transport handling). Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises stress under LC1-LC4 must remain below 247 MPa (370 / 1.5 yield FoS); (R2) under LC5 landing drop, stress below 370 MPa (yield, no permanent bend); (R3) A-arm dry mass must not exceed 2.5 kg; (R4) first arm natural frequency must exceed 80 Hz to decouple from wheel hop (~15 Hz) and drivetrain (~60 Hz); (R5) ball-joint mount vertical deflection under LC2 bump must remain below 2 mm to preserve kinematic accuracy; (R6) weld-toe fatigue check over 1e5 bump cycles (LC2 amplitude): alternating stress at weld toe must remain below endurance limit of 180 MPa (approx 0.5 x yield for welded mild steel) with 1.3 margin = 138 MPa. Thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling analyses not primary. Deliverables: STEP AP242 A-arm with two tube bodies, weld map, FEA per load case, fatigue margin, modal, mass.
short_prompt:
Design the front lower suspension A-arm of a collegiate off-road benchmark Brasil 2026 entry (RATBSB Emenda 7). Curb 275 kg + 90 kg driver; front weight distribution 42% (77 kg per front wheel); suspension travel +/- 150 mm. Secondary structural member per Baja T.9.2: welded AISI 1018 DOM 25.4 mm OD x 1.65 mm wall tubing.

Envelope: trapezoidal planform with 320 mm inner pivot axis and 400 mm outer ball-joint mount (30 deg included A angle).

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5 (allowable 247 MPa) on LC1-LC4; LC5 drop limited to yield (370 MPa); dry mass <= 2.5 kg; first natural frequency > 80 Hz (decouple from wheel hop ~15 Hz and drivetrain ~60 Hz); ball-joint vertical deflection under bump < 2 mm; welded-mild-steel weld-toe endurance 180 MPa x 1/1.3 = 138 MPa over 1e5 bump cycles.

Load cases.
- Static 1 g: 755 N up at the ball-joint.
- 3 g bump: 2266 N vertical.
- 1.5 g lateral + 1 g vertical cornering: 1133 N lateral + 755 N vertical.
- 1.2 g long decel + 1 g vertical braking: 906 N fore-aft + 755 N vertical (couple at inner pivots).
- 1 m landing drop: 5 g vertical = 3776 N at the ball-joint.
- Tie-down handling: 500 N up on the outer ball-joint.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Weld-toe fatigue is a post-process using stress outputs from LC2.

prompt.title: collegiate off-road benchmark Brasil 2026 Front Lower A-Arm
prompt.brief: AISI 1018 DOM welded A-arm for 77 kg corner load, collegiate off-road benchmark impact + fatigue gates.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam (BEAM188) for tubes, solid at weld-rich nodes

eval_coverage: collegiate off-road benchmark T.9.2 secondary-member traceability
